blurb: One significant headline-level signal this run: a US government export-control directive reportedly suspending access to Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models (announced Jun 12). Full text not captured — Anthropic's RSS feeds 404'd, so this is title-only pending a clean read.

publications.title: The US government has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
publications.summary: [Headline only — body not captured this run.] Anthropic's news page surfaces an announcement that the US government issued an export-control directive suspending all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5. If accurate, this is a material frontier-policy event (export controls reaching specific frontier models). Needs a full read before summarising substance; not fabricating detail beyond the headline.

publications.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Cadences
publications.summary: Latest Anthropic Economic Index — the lab's regular publication tracking how Claude is being used across the economy. Directly relevant to the AI-and-labour, productivity and adoption-evidence debates Ada Lovelace and others are pushing back on.

publications.title: Project Fetch: Phase two
publications.summary: Frontier Red Team update — Anthropic's safety-evaluations arm. The Red Team's output is some of the most directly policy-relevant work a frontier lab publishes, feeding evaluation methodologies that AISI and others draw on.

publications.title: Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise
publications.summary: Evidence on how Claude Code is changing returns to coding expertise — an empirical addition to the displacement-vs-augmentation debate that's animating UK and EU labour-market policy.

publications.title: Paving the way for agents in biology
publications.summary: Anthropic's work on biology agents — biosecurity policy material. Sits in the same debate as AISI's biorisk evaluations and CETAS's biosecurity work.

publications.title: Measuring LLMs' impact on N-day exploits
publications.summary: Frontier Red Team measurement of how LLMs change the economics of N-day vulnerability exploitation — feeds directly into NCSC/AISI cyber-policy thinking.

publications.title: Mapping AI-enabled cyber threats: Insights from the LLM ATT&CK Navigator
publications.summary: Frontier Red Team release of an LLM ATT&CK Navigator — methodology for systematically mapping AI-enabled cyber threats. Cyber-policy infrastructure.

publications.title: Teaching Claude why
publications.summary: Alignment research on reducing agentic misalignment — methodology relevant to the broader AI-safety policy debate about what 'aligned' actually looks like.

publications.title: Natural Language Autoencoders: Turning Claude's thoughts into text
publications.summary: Interpretability research — turning model internals into natural-language explanations. Interpretability is the technical underpinning of any transparency policy that requires explaining model behaviour.

roles.title: Anthropic Fellows Program, AI Safety
roles.location: London, UK; Ontario, CAN; Remote-Friendly, United States; San Francisco, CA

notes: [source feed broken] Seed blogRssUrl https://www.anthropic.com/news/rss.xml returns 404; https://www.anthropic.com/rss.xml also 404. Only https://www.anthropic.com/news (HTML) resolves, and readability captured a single item. Needs a working feed URL or a direct article URL. Flag to Techie for the ingestion pipeline (src/lib/rss). Until fixed, Anthropic coverage is degraded to headline-scraping.

news.title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
news.summary: Anthropic responds to a US government export-control directive suspending all access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models — a notable instance of frontier-model export controls hitting a specific company, and the first-party counterpart to the headline-only item logged earlier today.

news.title: Results from the first Anthropic Public Record
news.summary: Anthropic publishes results from its first Public Record — a transparency/governance exercise worth watching as a model for how labs document their own conduct.

news.title: Introducing Claude Corps
news.summary: A national fellowship for people early in their careers — an AI-workforce/talent-pipeline move with public-policy framing.

news.title: Anthropic opens Seoul office and announces new Korean AI partnerships
news.summary: Anthropic expands into South Korea — part of its international government/ecosystem footprint relevant to where frontier labs are placing policy bets.
